ai_hint: A shell is a page-level layout container that defines the spatial arrangement (header, nav, body, footer regions). Common shells: sidebar-main, full-bleed, top-nav-main, centered.

sidebar-main.name: Sidebar + Main
sidebar-main.description: Collapsible sidebar with header bar and scrollable main content area. Used by saas-dashboard, financial-dashboard, workbench, ecommerce (account pages).

sidebar-right.name: Right Sidebar
sidebar-right.description: Main content with a right-side navigation panel. Useful for settings, documentation sidebars, or inspector layouts.

code.example:
Shell({ config: 'sidebar-right' },
  Shell.Header({}, headerContent),
  Shell.Nav({}, navContent),
  Shell.Body({}, mainContent)
)

sidebar-main-footer.name: Sidebar + Main + Footer
sidebar-main-footer.description: Sidebar layout with a persistent footer bar. Used for apps needing status bars or action footers.

code.example:
Shell({ config: 'sidebar-main-footer' },
  Shell.Header({}, headerContent),
  Shell.Nav({}, navContent),
  Shell.Body({}, mainContent),
  Shell.Footer({}, footerContent)
)

sidebar-aside.name: Sidebar + Aside
sidebar-aside.description: Three-column layout with left navigation and right inspector/detail panel. Used for admin dashboards, email clients, IDE-style apps.

code.example:
Shell({ config: 'sidebar-aside' },
  Shell.Header({}, headerContent),
  Shell.Nav({}, navContent),
  Shell.Body({}, mainContent),
  Shell.Aside({}, asideContent)
)

top-nav-main.name: Top Nav + Main
top-nav-main.description: Horizontal navigation bar with full-width main content below. Used by ecommerce (storefront), portfolio, content-site.

top-nav-footer.name: Top Nav + Footer
top-nav-footer.description: Horizontal nav with main content and a persistent footer. Used for marketing sites, documentation with ToC footer.

code.example:
Shell({ config: 'top-nav-footer' },
  Shell.Header({}, headerContent),
  Shell.Nav({}, navItems),
  Shell.Body({}, mainContent),
  Shell.Footer({}, footerContent)
)

centered.name: Centered
centered.description: Centered card on a background. Used for auth flows (login, register, forgot password) across all archetypes.

full-bleed.name: Full Bleed
full-bleed.description: No persistent navigation. Scroll-driven hero-first layout. Used by portfolio landing pages.

minimal-header.name: Minimal Header
minimal-header.description: Slim header with centered content below. Used for checkout flows, focused task pages.

top-nav-sidebar.name: Top Nav + Sidebar
top-nav-sidebar.description: Full-width header spanning both columns, with left sidebar navigation below. Combines top branding with sidebar navigation depth.

code.example:
Shell({ config: 'top-nav-sidebar' },
  Shell.Header({}, headerContent),
  Shell.Nav({}, sidebarContent),
  Shell.Body({}, mainContent)
)

workspace-aside.name: Workspace Aside
workspace-aside.description: Three-column layout for collaborative editing with page tree navigation, document editor body, and comment/activity sidebar. Used by document-editor archetype for real-time collaboration.

terminal-split.name: Terminal Split
terminal-split.description: Terminal-style layout with status bar, resizable split panes, and hotkey bar. Optimized for CLI tool interfaces with monospace typography and ASCII borders.

chat-portal.name: Chat Portal
chat-portal.description: AI chatbot layout with collapsible conversation sidebar and anchored input. Used by ai-chatbot archetype for chat-first applications.
